TContainer owns/hosts slot layout and slot residency positions.
TContainer shape must match uniform container capacity profile.
TContainerRegistry stores indexed TContainer instances.
Registry capacity and generations follow active capacity/topology profiles.
BundleAgent owns/uses circulation capacity, uniform capacity, scaling profiles.
BundleAgent creates/manages the container family shape.
Bundle topology must be reflected in registry storage.
Horizontal/vertical scaling and profile switching are safe-point guarded.
Moderator manages readiness/reset surface for a concrete container.
Moderator identifies moderated container through registry index.
Moderator applies BundleAgent default empty profile aligned with active capacity.
Container reset must be safe-point guarded.
Reset follows release/recycle lifecycle.
Waiting lists store eligible container registry indices.
Waiting list admission depends on observed container state/readiness.
RoundManager registers eligible container indices into waiting lists.
RoundManager evaluates container state/readiness.
RoundManager candidate indices must resolve through registry.
Round closure/completion may create safe points.
ReferencePrecalculator consumes ordered waiting-list candidates.
ReferencePrecalculator resolves container registry indices.
ReferencePrecalculator checks container state/layout.
ReferencePrecalculator checks slot state and prepares slot access refs.
ReferencePrecalculator needs round scope/context.
References are invalidated across safe-point guarded transitions.
Ingress consumes TIngressAccessRef from ReferencePrecalculator.
Ingress places envelope into a slot via ref.
Ingress is reached through Log API/placement binding surface.
Dispatcher consumes TDispatchAccessRef from ReferencePrecalculator.
Dispatcher exposes dispatch-ready slot material via refs/views.
Dispatcher exposes through bridge-facing communication boundary.
Dispatch exposure participates in release/recycle lifecycle.
Exposure closure/release may create safe-point preconditions.
Release/recycle needs slot release states.
Release/recycle resets/reenters containers.
Release/recycle depends on consumed/invalidated refs.
Recycle/reset requires safe points.
Eviction candidate is a container with state/time range.
Eviction targets registry-indexed containers.
Eviction may use dispatch waiting-list ordering.
Eviction requires refs consumed/invalidated.
Eviction requires safe point.
Eviction is triggered by capacity boundary/profile.
Diagnostics are emitted as reports.
Diagnostics may consume views.
Communication binding describes ingress placement surface.
Communication binding describes dispatch output boundary.
Bindings expose safe views.
Bindings emit readiness/compatibility reports.
Read-side profile depends on bridge/post-bridge boundary contracts.
Read-side admission consumes post-dispatch exposure semantics.
Read-side profile needs post-bridge capacity/backpressure profiles.
Read-side profile may reuse/specialize container residency.
Read-side profile may reuse/specialize registry indexing.
Read-side failed dispatch/retry/backpressure lists reuse waiting-list family.
Read-side receiver-facing rounds may reuse round mechanics.
Read-side profile owns delayed release using release/recycle concepts.
Read-side delay/retry needs diagnostics.
Read-side capacity pressure may use eviction/capacity boundary policies.
